name: From Los Palacios y Villafranca to Las Cab
name_original: Desde Los Palacios y Villafranca hasta Las Cabezas de San Juan – circular
description: Enjoy an easy 31.4-mile road cycling route from Los Palacios y Villafranca to Las Cabezas de San Juan, featuring flat terrain and flamingo v
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Los Palacios Y Villafranca are set within the Guadalquivir-Doñana tourist region of Seville province. The area features flat to gently undulating landscapes, characterized by extensive agricultural plains and natural wetlands. These terrains provide diverse and engaging routes suitable for road cycling. The region's proximity to Seville also allows for interconnected routes and exploration of a wider network. Best road cycling routes around Los Palacios Y Villafranca The most popular road cycling route is Morón de la Frontera from Utrera – circular via El Coronil, a 53.0 miles (85.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the regional landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Los Palacios y Villafranca to Las Cab, an easy 31.4 miles (50.6 km) path. This route provides a straightforward ride through the local agricultural areas. Local road cyclists also love the Circular Route of Las Cabezas de San Juan, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail leading through agricultural plains, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Los Palacios Y Villafranca is defined by agricultural plains, natural wetlands, and sections of the La Corchuela Peri-urban Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Los Palacios Y Villafranca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Palacios Y Villafranca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Los Palacios y Villafranca?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Los Palacios y Villafranca, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. These include 5 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What is the general terrain like for road cycling in Los Palacios y Villafranca?
A: The terrain in Los Palacios y Villafranca is predominantly flat to gently undulating, characterized by extensive agricultural plains and natural wetlands. This makes it ideal for road cycling, allowing for smooth, long-distance rides through picturesque rural scenery. You'll also find sections that pass through areas like the La Corchuela Peri-urban Park.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the routes in Los Palacios y Villafranca?
A: The road cycling routes in Los Palacios y Villafranca are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Over 1,500 road cyclists have explored the area, often praising the diverse terrain and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Los Palacios y Villafranca offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An example is the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995745019/from-los-palacios-y-villafranca-to-las-cab" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Los Palacios y Villafranca to Las Cab&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 31.4-mile (50.6 km) path through local agricultural areas.

Q: Can I find challenging or long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking more challenging or longer rides, Los Palacios y Villafranca and its surroundings offer several options. A difficult route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995751329/from-utrera-to-villamartin-circular-via-el-coronil-and-puerto-serrano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Utrera to Villamartín – circular via El Coronil and Puerto Serrano&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 73 miles (118 km) with significant elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996059236/circular-route-of-las-cabezas-de-san-juan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of Las Cabezas de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.6-mile (54.1 km) trail through agricultural plains.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can explore diverse landscapes including agricultural plains and natural wetlands like the Cerro de las Cigüeñas and El Pantano. A significant natural feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela&lt;/a&gt;, which offers diverse ecosystems. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7382739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Metropolitan Green Corridor&lt;/a&gt;, a cycle path connecting various municipalities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Los Palacios y Villafranca?
A: The best time for road cycling in Los Palacios y Villafranca is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This allows for comfortable long rides through the open landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options?
A: Given the flat to gently undulating terrain and the presence of dedicated cycle paths like sections of the Metropolitan Green Corridor, many routes in Los Palacios y Villafranca can be considered family-friendly. Easy routes through agricultural areas are particularly suitable for families looking for a relaxed outing.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route?
A: Los Palacios y Villafranca, being a local town, offers various public parking options within the municipality. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot, as many routes begin from accessible points within or just outside the town where street parking or small public lots may be available.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Yes, as you cycle through Los Palacios y Villafranca and its surrounding towns, you'll find local cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can stop for refreshments. The agricultural plains often lead to small villages or towns that cater to locals and visitors, providing opportunities to refuel during your ride.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in certain areas?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and designated cycle paths in and around Los Palacios y Villafranca. However, if your route takes you through private land or specific protected natural reserves, it's always wise to check for any local regulations or signage.
tips: If you are lucky enough to be cycling here at the appropriate time, you will be able to stop to contemplate these peculiar beings, who stop "to refuel" at this stop on their migratory path.

Las Cabezas de San Juan is a municipality located in the Lower Guadalquivir and where we can enjoy the Baroque church of San Juan Bautista, which dominates the entire plain from the top of the town. The Plaza de los Mártires, in memory of Colonel Riego, is also worth a visit.

The church was built in the 15th century by the Duchy of Arcos, which had the dominion over the city of Los Palacios. The Ponce de León family felt a great devotion to the Virgen de las Nieves, patron saint of their city, so they dedicated the temple of Los Palacios to this Marian devotion. Inside, the organ stands out, built by Francisco de Ortiguez in 1747. In 2001, the palace sculptor and restorer Juan Manuel Martín rebuilt the old wooden monstrance from the seventeenth century, transforming it into a small temple that processions in the corpus, with an image of the Child Jesus. The church is in Baroque style with several neoclassical contributions. The highlight of the parish church is the main altarpiece, the work of the Flemish master painter Pablo Legot and dating from the seventeenth century (1631-36). The three canvases that make it up stand out: the central canvas representing "The Adoration of the Shepherds" and the side canvases with the two saints John, the Baptist and the Evangelist. The primitive altarpiece must have been modified in 1712 and definitively rebuilt in 1865.

faqs.question: How difficult is the circular road cycling route from Los Palacios y Villafranca to Las Cabezas de San Juan?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as easy, making it suitable for a wide range of road cyclists, including beginners. It features predominantly flat terrain with minimal elevation changes, ensuring a comfortable ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a very smooth ride with predominantly flat terrain. You'll cycle mainly on asphalt (approximately 49.4 km) and other paved roads (about 1.09 km). The path types include roads, state highways, and streets, all well-suited for a race bike.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy open views throughout the ride. A notable natural highlight is a great spot for flamingo watching, especially during their migratory stops. As you reach Las Cabezas de San Juan, you can visit the impressive Baroque Church of Santa María la Blanca and the Plaza de los Mártires.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Seville province generally offers pleasant cycling weather for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if cycling during those months.

faqs.question: Are there any specific permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It uses public roads and paths, making it freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily uses public roads and state highways, which are generally accessible, it's not specifically designed as a dog-friendly trail. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are comfortable with road cycling environments and always kept on a leash, especially given the traffic on some sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car at the start of the trail in Los Palacios y Villafranca?
faqs.answer: Los Palacios y Villafranca, being a town, offers various public parking options. You can typically find street parking or designated public parking areas within the town center or near its outskirts, which would be convenient for starting your circular ride.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section (approximately 4%) of this route overlaps with the historic Vía Augusta de Cádiz a Sevilla.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Los Palacios y Villafranca and Las Cabezas de San Juan are towns with various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments, a meal, or to refill water bottles.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given it's a road cycling route, we recommend bringing your race bike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles, snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a phone for navigation and emergencies. Sunscreen is also advisable, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the route is easy in terms of difficulty and elevation, it primarily uses roads and state highways. Families with children should assess their comfort level with cycling alongside traffic. For experienced young cyclists, it could be a pleasant ride, but parental supervision is essential.
